questions.q: Anna Mani is best remembered for her work in which branch of science?
questions.explanation: Anna Mani was a physicist and meteorologist who modernised India's weather instrumentation and measurement of solar radiation, ozone and wind.

questions.q: By what popular nickname is Anna Mani often known?
questions.explanation: Because of her pioneering contributions to Indian meteorology, Anna Mani is widely celebrated as 'The Weather Woman of India'.

questions.q: In which country was Anna Mani born and where did she carry out her career?
questions.explanation: Anna Mani was born in India (in present-day Kerala) and spent her career advancing Indian science.

questions.q: Anna Mani was especially noted for designing and standardising what kind of equipment?
questions.explanation: She specialised in meteorological instruments, including devices to measure solar radiation, ozone and wind.

questions.q: Solar radiation, which Anna Mani worked to measure, is energy that comes primarily from where?
questions.explanation: Solar radiation is the energy emitted by the Sun, a key quantity Anna Mani built instruments to measure.

questions.q: Anna Mani was a pioneering woman in Indian science during which broad period?
questions.explanation: Anna Mani (1918-2001) did her major scientific work through the middle and later decades of the 20th century.

questions.q: Atmospheric ozone, which Anna Mani studied and measured, is important because it does what?
options: Absorbs much of the Sun's ultraviolet radiation
questions.explanation: The ozone layer absorbs much of the Sun's harmful ultraviolet radiation, making ozone measurement important to atmospheric science.

questions.q: Which national weather agency is most closely associated with Anna Mani's career?
questions.explanation: Anna Mani spent much of her career at the India Meteorological Department (IMD), where she modernised weather instrumentation.

questions.q: Anna Mani's work on instruments was significant largely because it helped India do what?
options: Build its own scientific measuring tools rather than rely on imports
questions.explanation: She championed indigenous design and standardisation of meteorological instruments, reducing dependence on imported equipment.

questions.q: Renewable energy from which source, measured using radiation instruments of the kind Anna Mani developed, did her data help assess in India?
questions.explanation: Her measurements of solar radiation and wind provided foundational data later valuable for assessing India's solar and wind energy potential.

questions.q: In which Indian state, then part of the Travancore region, was Anna Mani born in 1918?
questions.explanation: Anna Mani was born in 1918 in the Travancore region, in present-day Kerala, into a Syrian Christian family.

questions.q: Anna Mani carried out early research in Bangalore under which renowned Indian Nobel laureate physicist?
questions.explanation: She worked under Nobel laureate C. V. Raman at the Indian Institute of Science in Bangalore, studying the optical properties of gemstones.

questions.q: Under C. V. Raman, Anna Mani's early research focused on the spectroscopy and optical properties of what?
questions.explanation: Her early research under Raman examined the luminescence and optical properties of diamonds and rubies.

questions.q: Where did Anna Mani go to study and specialise in meteorological instruments after her early work in India?
questions.explanation: She went to Imperial College, London, where she specialised in meteorological instruments before returning to India.

questions.q: Anna Mani joined the India Meteorological Department in which decade?
questions.explanation: She joined the India Meteorological Department in the late 1940s, around 1948, shortly after India's independence.

questions.q: What senior post did Anna Mani eventually rise to within the India Meteorological Department?
questions.explanation: Anna Mani rose to become a Deputy Director General of the India Meteorological Department, a senior leadership role.

questions.q: Although she pursued physics through to research level, Anna Mani did not receive a formal PhD largely because of what?
options: A technicality involving her lack of a master's degree
questions.explanation: Despite her substantial research under Raman, she was denied a PhD on a technicality related to not holding a formal master's degree.

questions.q: Anna Mani set up a workshop to manufacture which kind of devices indigenously in India?
questions.explanation: She established and oversaw the indigenous design and manufacture of meteorological instruments, improving their availability and standards in India.

questions.q: Instruments developed under Anna Mani's guidance measured radiation, wind, and which atmospheric gas linked to UV protection?
questions.explanation: She set up a network and instruments for measuring atmospheric ozone, in addition to solar radiation and wind.

questions.q: As a strong advocate for which energy source did Anna Mani's radiation measurements prove especially useful?
questions.explanation: Her detailed solar radiation measurements made her an early and important contributor to assessing India's solar energy potential.

questions.q: Anna Mani was a fellow of which prominent Indian scientific body?
questions.explanation: Anna Mani was honoured as a fellow of the Indian National Science Academy in recognition of her scientific contributions.

questions.q: Anna Mani's instrumentation work supported accurate forecasting and study of which seasonal phenomenon vital to India?
questions.explanation: Reliable weather instruments are essential for monitoring and forecasting India's monsoon, a focus of the meteorological work she advanced.

questions.q: Reflecting her work on atmospheric ozone, Anna Mani became a member of which international scientific body?
questions.explanation: Through her expertise in measuring atmospheric ozone, including work on ozone-sounding instruments, Anna Mani became a member of the International Ozone Commission.

questions.q: Anna Mani authored a well-known handbook in 1980 that dealt with which subject?
options: Solar radiation data and instruments for India
Marine fish of the Indian Ocean
questions.explanation: She wrote authoritative works such as 'Handbook for Solar Radiation Data for India' (1980), compiling key radiation measurements.

questions.q: After retiring from the IMD, Anna Mani was associated with the Raman Research Institute in which city?
questions.explanation: In her later years she was associated with the Raman Research Institute in Bangalore, the city where her career began under C. V. Raman.

questions.q: Anna Mani's foundational measurements of wind speed across India later proved valuable for what modern application?
questions.explanation: Her systematic wind measurements provided baseline data useful for assessing sites for wind energy generation in India.

questions.q: In which year did Anna Mani pass away?
questions.explanation: Anna Mani died in 2001, having shaped Indian meteorology over more than five decades.

questions.q: Which statement best captures a defining feature of Anna Mani's career as a woman scientist in India?
options: She rose to senior scientific leadership in a male-dominated field
She abandoned physics for the arts
questions.explanation: Anna Mani broke barriers by attaining senior leadership in meteorology at a time when few Indian women held such scientific positions.

questions.q: Anna Mani's emphasis on careful standardisation and calibration of instruments was important chiefly because it ensured what?
options: Reliable, comparable and accurate weather data
questions.explanation: Standardisation and calibration ensure that measurements are accurate and comparable across stations, the backbone of trustworthy meteorology.

questions.q: Anna Mani helped establish India's network of stations to measure solar radiation, making the country an early contributor to what kind of global science?
questions.explanation: By building a national radiation and ozone measurement network, she made India an early contributor to atmospheric and climate-related observation.
